sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Ms. Sanchez is a 59-year-old female who underwent mitral valve repair on 8/3/2020.
She was doing well at her previous follow-up on 9/17, and we recommended continuing her current medication regimen.
She has a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma.
She experiences recurrent chest discomfort with negative cardiac workup for coronary artery disease.
She reports feeling better since the surgery, but experiences occasional shallow breathing and chest pains that resolve with rest.
These episodes last a few minutes and recur a few times a month.
She denies any significant side effects from her medications, but notes that atenolol initially made her irritable.
Past medical history: Ms. Sanchez has a history of mitral regurgitation, atrial fibrillation, diabetes, and asthma.
She also has a history of recurrent chest discomfort with negative cardiac workup for coronary artery disease.
Review of systems: She experiences occasional shallow breathing and chest pains, which resolve with rest.
Current medications: I prescribed Coumadin 4mg daily, Lasix 4mg daily, and Atenolol 50mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has a head and neck examination with no jugular venous distension detected.
Lungs have reduced breath sounds, but auscultation and percussion are clear.
The patient's heart rhythm is irregularly irregular, consistent with atrial fibrillation.
S1 is slightly accentuated, and there is no S3.
The patient has trace peripheral edema on extremities.
Test Results: ECG taken on 12/26/2020 reveals atrial fibrillation with a controlled ventricular response and T-wave inversion anteriorly, with no significant changes compared to the previous study.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient is recovering well from mitral valve repair, but continues to experience atrial fibrillation with controlled ventricular response.
Problem list: Atrial fibrillation; Mitral regurgitation; Diabetes; Asthma; Recurrent chest discomfort; Trace peripheral edema.
Plan:
I will continue the patient's current medication regimen, including Coumadin 4mg daily, Lasix 4mg daily, and Atenolol 50mg daily.
I will also advise the patient to continue to be active, as this will help with her recovery.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in 6-9 months for follow-up.
